name: DropdownSorting
description: Sort-settings panel mounted in the Sort FilterChip's value popover. Two side-by-side <Dropdown> selects: Column (pick sortable parameter from the columns array) and Sort (Ascending / Descending). Pure state-staging surface — onColumnChange / onDirectionChange callbacks fire on selection.

props.name: columns
props.description: Available columns to sort by. SortColumn = { value: string; label: string }.

props.name: column
props.description: Currently selected column value. Defaults to columns[0].value when not provided.

props.name: onColumnChange
props.description: Called when the Column dropdown's selection changes

props.name: onDirectionChange
props.description: Called when the Sort direction dropdown's selection changes

states.rationale: The panel itself is a passive floating container. Each composed <Dropdown> select (Column + Direction) carries its own state contract — overlay hover on the trigger, focus-visible ring, listbox open/close on chevron click, ListItem selected highlight via info-subtle fill.

keyboard.Tab: Move focus between the two Dropdowns (Column → Direction) and out to neighbors
keyboard.Enter: On a focused Dropdown trigger: open the listbox. On a focused option: select it.
keyboard.Space: Same as Enter for native buttons
keyboard.ArrowUp / ArrowDown: Within an open listbox: move highlight to previous/next option

accessibility.focusManagement: Root is a passive <div role="dialog">. The two composed <Dropdown>s own focus management — each trigger is a native <button> with aria-haspopup="listbox" and aria-expanded; each listbox has role="listbox" with aria-selected on options. Tab navigates Column → Direction in visual order.

propertyMapping.note: Left <Dropdown> with label "Column". Options come from the columns prop. column + onColumnChange control the value.
Right <Dropdown> with label "Sort". Hard-coded two options: Ascending / Descending.

useWhen: Inside the value-side popover of the Sort FilterChip in the Filters row of a TableTopBar
Any compact 'sort by column + direction' picker where the consumer owns the staging state
doNotUseFor: Multi-column sorting (sort by name asc, then date desc) — not supported by this component

knownDeviations.description: Spec mentions --dls-color-component-dropdown-sorting-* tokens but they do not exist in tokens.json. CSS uses semantic surface/border tokens directly; the composed Dropdowns carry their own token references.
